project_name: Pilot-scale MnO2 nanoparticle production for battery cathode components
process_goal: Scale up a lab-scale hydrothermal synthesis of MnO2 nanostructures into a small pilot-scale batch process and recommend preliminary equipment.

product_summary.name: MnO2 nanoparticle powder
critical_quality_attributes: controlled nanoscale particle size or grain size
low unwanted metallic contamination from equipment

preferred_materials: ceramic or polymeric filter media with low contamination risk
avoid_materials: unlined carbon steel in product-contact areas
materials that may leach unwanted transition metals
known_risks: Hydrothermal operation requires pressure-rated sealed equipment.
Temperature ramp, hold temperature, and cooling profile may affect MnO2 morphology and crystallinity.
Fine MnO2 particles may be difficult to separate and may clog filters.
Washing efficiency affects residual K+, sulfate, acid, and soluble ion contamination.
Equipment contact materials may introduce unwanted metallic contamination.
Drying temperature and vacuum conditions may affect hydration state or powder properties.
Batch-to-batch reproducibility is important for battery material quality.
missing_or_uncertain_data: Hydrothermal operating pressure is autogenous and given as a condition, not a confirmed numeric value. Design pressure at the hold temperature must be verified.
Particle size, yield, and morphology after scale-up are not predicted by this tool and require experimental confirmation.

related_concerns: Washing efficiency affects residual K+, sulfate, acid, and soluble ion contamination.
Equipment contact materials may introduce unwanted metallic contamination.
Washing efficiency affects residual K+, sulfate, acid, and soluble ion contamination.
Equipment contact materials may introduce unwanted metallic contamination.

Temperature ramp, hold temperature, and cooling profile may affect MnO2 morphology and crystallinity.
Drying temperature and vacuum conditions may affect hydration state or powder properties.

accepted_candidates.equipment_name: Stirred pressure reactor with PTFE-lined wetted parts

unresolved_risks: Pressure rating must be verified at 180 C with autogenous pressure.
Thermal ramp and cooling profile may affect MnO2 morphology.

accepted_candidates.equipment_name: Stirred pressure reactor with PTFE-lined wetted parts

unresolved_risks: Pressure rating must be verified at 180 C with autogenous pressure.
Thermal ramp and cooling profile may affect MnO2 morphology.

accepted_candidates.equipment_name: Lab or pilot vacuum filtration setup

recommended_equipment.equipment_name: Lab or pilot vacuum filtration setup

unresolved_risks: Filter media pore size and cake permeability must be tested.
May need pre-wetting or staged filtration.

accepted_candidates.equipment_name: Repeated DI water reslurry washing vessel

recommended_equipment.equipment_name: Repeated DI water reslurry washing vessel

unresolved_risks: Conductivity and pH should be monitored to confirm washing completeness.
Avoid product loss during repeated washing.
Drying temperature and time should be validated against MnO2 phase and hydration requirements.
Use clean, dedicated containers to avoid cross-contamination.
Consider powder handling PPE and dust control.

strengths: Good for glass and PTFE lab-scale vessels
limitations: Not suitable for pressure-rated hydrothermal reactors
rfq_notes: Ask for working volume, jacket temperature range, stirrer material, and product-contact material certification.

suppliers.example_equipment: 316L stainless steel jacketed stirred tank, 5-50 L
strengths: Custom agitator and vessel geometry options
limitations: Product-contact metal contamination must be assessed
May require passivation and cleaning validation
rfq_notes: Ask about surface finish, passivation, cleanability, and compatibility with acidic manganese-containing aqueous slurry.

strengths: Good for glass and PTFE lab-scale vessels
limitations: Not suitable for pressure-rated hydrothermal reactors

suppliers.example_equipment: Teflon-lined stainless steel batch autoclave, 0.5-5 L
rfq_notes: Ask for certified pressure rating at 180 C, maximum fill fraction, liner material, pressure relief options, and temperature control method.

suppliers.example_equipment: Stirred pressure reactor with PTFE-lined wetted parts, 2-20 L
strengths: Instrumentation options for pressure and temperature monitoring
limitations: Agitator and seals require material compatibility review
rfq_notes: Ask for wetted material options, seal design, agitation range, pressure relief system, data logging, and batch reproducibility features.

suppliers.example_equipment: Teflon-lined stainless steel batch autoclave, 0.5-5 L

suppliers.example_equipment: Stirred pressure reactor with PTFE-lined wetted parts, 2-20 L

limitations: Agitator and seals require material compatibility review
rfq_notes: Ask for wetted material options, seal design, agitation range, pressure relief system, data logging, and batch reproducibility features.

recommended_equipment.equipment_name: Lab or pilot vacuum filtration setup

suppliers.example_equipment: Lab or pilot vacuum filtration setup with ceramic or PTFE-compatible media
strengths: Good for washing and low-contamination handling
Low cost for MVP bench-pilot scale
limitations: Fine nanoparticles may blind filter media
rfq_notes: Ask for filter media pore size, chemical compatibility, hold-up volume, cake washing options, and expected filtration area.

suppliers.example_equipment: Pilot basket centrifuge for fine slurry separation, 1-20 L
strengths: Suitable for fine particle slurry dewatering
Better scale fit than bench-top centrifuges
rfq_notes: Ask for bowl material, liner options, minimum particle retention, cleaning procedure, and compatibility with MnO2 nanoparticle slurry.

recommended_equipment.equipment_name: Repeated DI water reslurry washing vessel

suppliers.example_equipment: Lab or pilot vacuum filtration setup with ceramic or PTFE-compatible media
strengths: Good for washing and low-contamination handling
Low cost for MVP bench-pilot scale
limitations: Fine nanoparticles may blind filter media
rfq_notes: Ask for filter media pore size, chemical compatibility, hold-up volume, cake washing options, and expected filtration area.

suppliers.example_equipment: Vacuum oven for battery material powder drying, 30-120 C
strengths: Simple and suitable for bench-pilot MVP
rfq_notes: Ask for chamber material, shelf temperature uniformity, vacuum range, tray material, and contamination control features.

suppliers.example_equipment: Small tray dryer or convection oven for pilot powder drying
strengths: Lower cost than integrated filter dryers
limitations: May be less suitable for high-purity battery powders
rfq_notes: Ask for temperature uniformity, HEPA filtration options, tray material, and cleaning protocol.

suppliers.example_equipment: Clean HDPE, polypropylene, or glass powder collection containers
strengths: Easy to dedicate containers to one material
limitations: Static charge and dust control may require additional handling procedures
rfq_notes: Ask for cleanroom packaging, material certification, container volume, and antistatic options.

capex_summary.note: Illustrative mock CAPEX only. Each distinct recommended equipment item is counted once (equipment shared across steps is not double-counted). Totals use the lowest and highest mock supplier prices. Not a quote.

rfq_draft.intro: Request for preliminary quotation for pilot-scale equipment supporting the production of MnO2 nanoparticle powder for aqueous zinc-ion battery cathode component. Target batch size is 2 L in bench-pilot batch operation. This is an early-stage inquiry for budgetary purposes only.

operating_conditions: Operating temperature is 25 C, so the equipment must be rated to cover this point with margin (recommended example is rated 20 to 90 C).
Operating pressure is about 1 bar, so the equipment must be rated to cover this with adequate relief (recommended example is rated 1 to 1 bar).
Batch volume is 2 L, so the working volume must accommodate this (recommended example handles 0.5 to 5 L).
preferred_contact_materials: ceramic or polymeric filter media with low contamination risk
materials_to_avoid: unlined carbon steel in product-contact areas
materials that may leach unwanted transition metals
questions_for_supplier: What product-contact materials are available, and can they avoid: unlined carbon steel in product-contact areas, materials that may leach unwanted transition metals, porous or difficult-to-clean product-contact surfaces?
Can the equipment meet the operating conditions listed above with an adequate safety margin?
What contamination control, cleaning, and validation options are supported?
What is the budgetary price and typical lead time?
(BenchScale Process Glassware Co.) Ask for working volume, jacket temperature range, stirrer material, and product-contact material certification.
(PilotMix Systems) Ask about surface finish, passivation, cleanability, and compatibility with acidic manganese-containing aqueous slurry.

operating_conditions: Operating temperature is 180 C, so the equipment must be rated to cover this point with margin (recommended example is rated 80 to 220 C).

Batch volume is 2 L, so the working volume must accommodate this (recommended example handles 0.1 to 5 L).
